items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs first round schedule television results
items.description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs are underway. Here is the complete schedule for the first round of the playoffs, including TV information and results:

items.headline: Montreal Canadiens Yvan Cournoyer torch Game 3 Lightning
items.description:
When you need a little extra speed, you go to the Roadrunner.
Yvan Cournoyer , a 10-time Stanley Cup champion forward with the Montreal Canadiens nicknamed "The Roadrunner" for his skating speed, carried the team torch into Bell Centre ahead of Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round against

items.headline: Utah Mammoth Colorado Avalanche 2027 Winter Classic set for New Years Eve
items.description:
SALT LAKE CITY -- It will be a New Year’s Eve party and more when the Utah Mammoth host the Colorado Avalanche in the 2027 Discover NHL Winter Classic.
NHL Commissioner Gary Bettman announced Friday that the date of the game will be Dec. 31, 2026, and he gave the

items.headline: Youppi gifts Cole Caufield dad Montreal Canadiens hats Game 3
items.description:
Don’t worry Cole Caufield, Youppi has your dad covered.
The Montreal Canadiens mascot gifted the star forward’s dad, Paul, team hats before Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round against the Tampa Bay Lightning at Bell Centre on Friday.
In a video posted by the team on social media,

items.headline: Dallas Stars Minnesota Wild Game 4 preview April 25, 2026
items.description:

items.description:

items.headline: Minnesota Wild seek spark from Kirill Kaprizov, power play in Game 4
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- The Minnesota Wild have matched the Dallas Stars stride for stride at even strength in the Western Conference First Round, meeting the physical demands and pace through three games of a rugged best-of-7 series in the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
They've won board battles, received timely goaltending

items.headline: Short Shifts Power Rankings April 24 2026
items.description: Throw out the Xs and Os and join Short Shifts for our own weekly power rankings. What is our criteria? Whatever makes us smile, laugh, cry or any other wholesome feeling. This list is updated every Friday with our top moments of the week.

items.headline: Miro Heiskanen providing big boost for Dallas Stars in playoffs
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- It was only one week ago that Miro Heiskanen was dealing with a lower-body injury and the Dallas Stars didn't know if their star defenseman was going to be healthy enough to start the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
Now it almost feels like that never happened.
Heiskanen

items.headline: Edmonton Oilers Anaheim Ducks Game 3 preview April 24 2026
items.description:
(2P) Oilers at (3P) Ducks
Western Conference First Round Game 3
Best-of-7 series tied 1-1
10 p.m. ET; HBO MAX, Victory+, truTV, TNT, KCOP-13, SN, TVAS, CBC

items.headline: Evgeni Malkin hoping to extend series vs Flyers return to Penguins next season
items.description:
VOORHEES, N.J. -- Like many of his teammates, Evgeni Malkin came off the ice smiling after the Pittsburgh Penguins practice at Flyers Training Center on Friday.
The Penguins’ mood was upbeat despite the dire situation they face, trailing the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0 in the Eastern Conference First Round and trying

items.headline: Los Angeles Kings look to keep season and Anze Kopitar career going
items.description:
LOS ANGELES -- It can’t end yet, not this way, not this early.
The Los Angeles Kings aren’t ready for an early exit in the Stanley Cup Playoffs. They want to keep it going for each other, for their organization, for their fans. But most of all they want to

items.headline: Colorado Avalanche spreading wealth on offense vs Los Angeles Kings
items.description:
LOS ANGELES -- Nathan MacKinnon is looking for his first goal of the Stanley Cup Playoffs. So is fellow Colorado Avalanche forward Nazem Kadri. Defenseman Cale Makar got his first goal of the postseason on Thursday.
Concerning? Nope, not one iota. One of the Avalanche’s biggest assets is their depth

items.headline: Senators Jake Sanderson Artem Zub out for game 4 against Hurricanes in NHL playoffs
items.description:
OTTAWA -- It's a case of déjà vu for the Ottawa Senators.
For the second straight spring, the Senators find themselves staring at a 3-0 first-round series deficit. Except this time around, the mountain may be even steeper to climb.
